title: Tile factory PMTiles conformance cases
description: Declarative checks for the future binary conformance PMTiles archive. The archive itself is deferred until the proposed toolchain and repository exemption are ratified.

cases.description: Validate the artifact manifest against the artifact manifest schema before inspecting an archive.
cases.expected: The manifest has no schema violations and contains no pending-build sentinels for a publish candidate.

cases.description: Compare PMTiles vector layer metadata and decoded sample tiles with the manifest inventory.
cases.expected: Every declared layer_id is present and no undeclared layer id is encoded.

cases.description: For each layer, inspect representative occupied tiles at every integer zoom from minzoom through maxzoom, including the declared geographic extremities.
cases.expected: Coverage exists throughout each declared zoom range, no features occur below minzoom, and archive metadata does not claim a maxzoom below the layer contract.

cases.description: Decode a deterministic sample from every layer, including boundary and sparse tiles, and inspect every feature in that sample.
cases.expected: Every required attribute is present with the contracted scalar type; non-nullable attributes are neither absent nor empty.

cases.description: Calculate SHA-256 over the exact PMTiles bytes selected for publication.
cases.expected: The lowercase digest exactly matches checksum.value in the manifest and checksum.status is verified.

cases.description: Inspect polygon rings crossing horizontal and vertical vector-tile boundaries at minzoom, maxzoom, and one intermediate zoom.
cases.expected: Reassembled geometries show no cracks, overlaps introduced by clipping, or missing slivers at tile seams within the simplification tolerance recorded by the future build.

cases.description: Inspect adjacent polygons that share source boundaries, including HUC8 and watershed-group neighbors, before and after tile clipping.
cases.expected: Shared edges remain coincident within the future build tolerance; polygons are valid and do not gain self-intersections, ring reversals, or artificial gaps.

cases.description: Inspect deterministic multipart, island, enclave, and interior-ring fixtures from each applicable source layer.
cases.expected: Parts and meaningful holes survive encoding at contracted zooms, subject only to an explicitly approved and recorded minimum-area policy.

cases.description: Inspect geometries nearest longitude -180 and 180 and render the archive across a wrapped world boundary.
cases.expected: No wraparound spikes, duplicate world-spanning polygons, or invalid long edges are introduced.

cases.description: Build twice from byte-identical inputs with the same pinned tools and declared parameters.
cases.expected: Archive SHA-256 digests match; otherwise publication is blocked until nondeterminism is explained and governed.
